metadata.description: Production-grade standards for Claude Code — Flutter, API (NestJS + Prisma), Web (Next.js), Mobile-universal. Install a pack, Claude applies the standards. No CLAUDE.md edits. v0.16.0 adds the honesty contract: no unsourced claims about the codebase, evidence required before declaring done, paired with a Stop hook that surfaces violations (cite-or-read).

plugins.name: core-hooks
plugins.description: Hooks-only plugin. SessionStart surfaces plugin-hook mechanics and (opt-in) a mandatory-skills preamble. PreToolUse blocks edits to secrets, dangerous shell, raw design values in token-managed projects, cold Task/Agent spawns below warmth threshold, and — when enforcement mode is opted in via .claude/enforcement.json — a per-pack rule registry. PostToolUse runs post-test on every Write/Edit/MultiEdit (detects stack, runs related fast tests, exit 2 on failure forces fix before turn proceeds; fail-open on detection errors). Stop hook blocks turn-end until each mandatory pack's gate has passed and verify-state.json has no unresolved tool FAILs, plus surfaces honesty violations (cite-or-read: factual claims about files not Read/Grep'd in the same turn — non-blocking WARN to stderr).

plugins.name: core-standards
plugins.description: Cross-stack engine, universal skills, and the production autonomous pipeline. Engine: verify-changes, codebase-index, planning, universal-rules, verification, auth-flows, craft-config, mcp-integration, docs-sync, subagent-brief. Honesty contract (always-loaded): no unsourced claims about the codebase, evidence-before-done — paired with the cite-or-read Stop hook in core-hooks. Autonomous pipeline (single prompt → verified delivery): spec-validator (challenges spec, locks acceptance-tests.md), contracts (architect-defined interfaces locked before any code), challenger (fresh-context adversarial review, 3-round cap), contract-tests (tests generated from contracts, implementer-locked), integration (wires implementations, runs acceptance tests, 5-attempt fix loop), full-setup (one-shot project layer generator). Slash commands: /full-setup, /spec.

plugins.name: flutter-standards
plugins.description: Flutter-specific standards — engineering (Dart, widget rules, data layer, api-data patterns), accessibility (Semantics, screen readers, 48dp touch targets), performance (DevTools, frame budget, const widgets). app-audit (full Flutter audit: pre-ship checks + craft quality + screen states). scaffold (feature + screen file structure generator). scan (hardcoded values, duplicate code, a11y patterns). Pair with mobile-standards for craft-guide, production-readiness, forms, and observability.

plugins.name: mobile-standards
plugins.description: Mobile-universal standards — craft quality (iOS Liquid Glass, Material You, tonal elevation, haptic timing), production readiness, forms, observability, testing, premium-signals (Spring animation values, bottom-sheet detents, scroll-linked animation). Applies to Flutter, React Native, SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose. Auto-invoked by description triggers.

plugins.name: api-standards
plugins.description: API pack — NestJS + Prisma. Auto-invoke: nestjs + code-quality standards; cross-stack-contracts (active when craft.json stacks has 2+ entries: unified error shape, cursor pagination, Bearer header, versioned routes, OpenAPI spec); websockets (conditional on socket.io/ws usage: connection auth, backoff reconnect, event enum, schema versioning). Sync-migrate workflow.

plugins.name: web-standards
plugins.description: Web pack — Next.js + Tailwind + shadcn. Auto-invoke: nextjs + production-readiness + craft-guide standards; premium-signals (market-sourced precise values — 2-layer shadow formula, OKLCH dark gray scale, Linear easing, tabular-nums, display tracking); i18n (conditional on next-i18next/react-i18next or locales/ dir). Audit commands delegate to core-standards:verify-changes. extract-tokens writes token source of truth.

plugins.name: design-standards
plugins.description: Platform-agnostic design pack — applies to Web, iOS, and Android. accessibility (WCAG 2.2), information-architecture, aesthetic-coherence (named rule refs — craft-guide:aesthetic-coherence), design-laws, brand-research, creative-direction.
